chunks.title: Discussions on Seed Oils and Nutrition
chunks.summary: The speaker shares insights from a recent meeting at the White House regarding the challenges of eliminating seed oils from diets and emphasizes the importance of grassroots education in improving health. He shares his perspective on the connection between diet and mental clarity.

chunks.title: Endless Summer and Dietary Perspectives
chunks.summary: A participant asks about the implications of an endless summer diet, prompting a discussion on how living in tropical climates may influence dietary choices. The speaker reflects on personal experiences living in warm environments and their impact on health.
chunks.transcript: >> Hey, Paul. Um I was wondering from a quantum biology perspective um what you think about the constant summer signals of having fruit all year long without any breaks. >> Yeah. Did Jack Pruce send you? >> Yes. Yes, >> we have an interloper. No, this is an interesting question. And a couple people asked me that over the course of this weekend. So the first level of answer to this question is that I'm basically live in endless summer because I live in the tropics, right? So I think humans evolved here and um this is where our bi biology began. I mean I spent time with the Hadza in Africa and um Tanzania is essentially the same latitude in the below the equator to the south and um so I think that it's pretty clear that humans began in a place like this within 10 degrees north or south of the equator. So, our biology was shaped by endless summer. Now, whether that that means we should always have endless summer now is remains to be seen. But I think for me personally, um I choose to live in these places. I may spend a little bit of time in Miami, but I'm not going to get much further from the equator. So, I don't think about it much because if I'm going to places that are further from the equator, like Washington DC, there such a micro um journey uh that it's not a big deal for me. I think people who live in Seattle or other climates might experiment with this. And I think that the best signals you're going to get are your own biology, how you sleep, your libido, your mood, your body composition. And you can get labs. And those will be mirrored in your own personal biology. You'll see it in your body fat composition, all these other things. Maybe the hormones, you know, testosterone, estradiol, SHPG, sex hormone, binding globulin. And I think thyroid is important to look at also. So whenever people are talking about fasting and the rem removal of carbohydrates, I just think it's important that you watch your thyroid and your body temperature. And you know, who knows um if we need to drop our body temperature and and turn down our metabolism at times. I I think these remain unanswered questions in the level of like really really granular um molecular medicine or quantum biology. But my concern for a lot of people with keto, strict carnivore or fasting is that you will see the thyroid hormones go in the wrong direction and you will see body temp go down. So there are some pretty well-known longevity fitness influencers who are quite um pale. Like you don't want to boast about your glacial body temperature. that that to me is an indication that your body is slowing down and that your metabolism is slowing down. And I think that um I I tend to lean more toward this sort of rate perspective that you want your body temperature to be high and you want your thyroid to be basically happy and partying all the time. He encourages participants to apply their knowledge, stay connected, and continue fostering the animal-based lifestyle, emphasizing the need for deliberate choices and resilience in the face of societal pressures.
